name: Intune Log Collector
description: Intune Log Collector is an Azure-based solution that collects logs from Intune-managed devices, including files, directories, and event logs. It deploys a Function App, Storage Account, and Key Vault, with the Remediation script driving log collection per LogsGatherRules.json and uploading a compressed archive to the logs container. Deployments support Azure Template Spec (Bicep/ARM) or direct ARM templates, with optional UI and post-deployment steps.
keywords: how to gather logs from managed devices
author: Nickolaj Andersen

securityCheck.aiSummary: The Intune Log Collector code uses standard authentication flows (Managed Identity for AAD tokens) and Intune/Graph integrations with device attestation. Primary concerns are around data exposure via blob content in responses and SAS token generation for storage access. No obfuscated code, no hardcoded secrets visible, and no evident credential theft, remote code execution, or malware-like patterns. Actionable mitigations focus on restricting blob data exposure and tightening SAS token scopes and lifetimes to minimize risk if tokens are leaked.

noObfuscatedCode.reason: Base64 decode and execute in Function App/modules/AADDeviceTrust.FunctionApp/Public/Get-AzureADDeviceAlternativeSecurityIds.ps1 ([lines: 27](https://github.com/MSEndpointMgr/IntuneLogCollector/blob/main/Function App/modules/AADDeviceTrust.FunctionApp/Public/Get-AzureADDeviceAlternativeSecurityIds.ps1#L27))
